title: AMPLIFAI CHALLENGE
description:
Annotated Multi-Phase Liver Imaging For Artificial Intelligence.
The AMPLIFAI challenge focuses on automated characterization of hepatocellular carcinoma (HCC) lesions in multi-phase abdominal CT using standardized, clinical imaging-based LI-RADS categories.
description_zh:
人工智能用多期肝脏成像注释数据

AMPLIFAI挑战赛聚焦于使用标准化的、基于临床成像的LI-RADS分类，对多期腹部CT中肝细胞癌(HCC)病灶进行自动表征。

pages.title: Overview
pages.content: ![](https://ihc.umd.edu/wp-content/uploads/2022/05/IHC-Horizontal-Logo-for-header.svg) ![](https:(https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/en/thumb/7/7c/University_of_Maryland_Medical_Center_logo.svg/960px-University_of_Maryland_Medical_Center_logo.svg.png) ## Welcome Welcome to the **Annotated Multi-Phase Liver Imaging For Artificial Intelligence (AMPLIFAI)** challenge. AMPLIFAI is a MICCAI 2026 challenge focused on automated characterization of hepatocellular carcinoma (HCC) lesions in multi-phase abdominal CT. Participants are tasked with classifying liver lesions according to the LI-RADS scoring system: a standardized, imaging-based framework used clinically to stratify the probability of HCC without requiring tissue biopsy. ## What is HCC? Hepatocellular carcinoma is the most common primary liver cancer and a leading cause of cancer-related mortality worldwide. It arises predominantly in patients with chronic liver disease or cirrhosis, a population that undergoes routine surveillance imaging, making CT a natural screening tool. Crucially, HCC is one of the few malignancies where diagnosis can be established entirely from imaging, no biopsy required. This is codified in the LI-RADS (Liver Imaging Reporting and Data System) framework, which assigns lesions a category from LR-1 ("definitely benign") to LR-5 ("definitely HCC") based on morphology and dynamic contrast enhancement patterns across multiple CT phases. An LR-5 designation alone is sufficient for clinical diagnosis and treatment planning. ## LI-RADS Framework The [LI-RADS](https://radiologyassistant.nl/abdomen/liver/li-rads) framework follows a strict clinical logic consisting of temporal analysis in multi-phase CT, where multiple contrast phases are acquired: before contrast injection (non-contrast) and at successive time points after injection (arterial, portal venous, and delayed) to capture dynamic enhancement patterns in the lesion. The primary gating feature is arterial phase hyperenhancement (APHE), where the lesion appears brighter than the surrounding healthy liver tissue in the early arterial phase. It is considered “non-rim” if the enhancement spreads internally and is not limited to the outer edge of the lesion. Only lesions exhibiting unequivocal non-rim APHE are considered for the highest probability categories (LR-3 to LR-5). If non-rim APHE is present, the LI-RADS category is determined by a combination of the lesion’s size and how non-rim APHE interacts with two major features: First, non-peripheral washout, where the lesion becomes darker than the liver in the portal venous or delayed phases. Second, enhancing capsule, where a thin, peripheral rim remains enhanced in the portal venous or delayed phases due to contrast retention. The lesion size and presence of one or both features increases the probability for HCC. For example, a lesion with non-rim APHE that is 2.3 cm and has one additional feature (washout or capsule) is categorized as LR-5 (“definitely HCC”) while a lesion without non-rim APHE can only reach a maximum of LR-4 (“probable HCC”), even if it is large and shows washout. ## Challenge Aim By benchmarking models against this clinically validated, multi-class diagnostic framework, AMPLIFAI aims to accelerate the development of trustworthy and interpretable AI systems for liver cancer diagnosis, helping bridge the gap between algorithmic performance and real-world clinical deployment. Participants will be provided with training data consisting of multi-phase CT volumes, lesion-level LI-RADS category labels, and voxel-level segmentations. Models will be evaluated solely on their ability to predict the correct LI-RADS category, enabling a focus on robust and clinically relevant classification performance.
pages.content_zh:
# 欢迎

欢迎来到**标注型多阶段肝脏影像用于人工智能(AMPLIFAI)**挑战赛。AMPLIFAI是一项MICCAI 2026挑战赛，专注于多阶段腹部CT中肝细胞癌(HCC)病灶的自动表征。参赛者的任务是根据LI-RADS评分系统对肝脏病灶进行分类：这是一套临床应用的标准化影像框架，用于评估HCC的概率，无需组织活检。

## 什么是HCC?

肝细胞癌是最常见的原发性肝癌，也是全球癌症相关死亡的主要原因。它主要发生在慢性肝病或肝硬化患者中，这类患者需要定期进行监测影像检查，使CT成为一种天然的筛查工具。至关重要的是，HCC是少数几种可以完全通过影像学确诊的恶性肿瘤之一，无需活检。这在LI-RADS(肝脏影像报告和数据系统)框架中有明确规定，该框架根据病灶形态和多个CT阶段的动态对比增强模式，将病灶分为LR-1("明确良性")到LR-5("明确HCC")等级。单独的LR-5等级足以用于临床诊断和治疗规划。

## LI-RADS框架

LI-RADS框架遵循严格的临床逻辑，包括多阶段CT的时间序列分析，其中采集多个对比阶段：注射对比剂前(非对比)和注射后的连续时间点(动脉期、门脉期和延迟期)，以捕捉病灶的动态增强模式。主要评判特征是动脉期高增强(APHE)，病灶在早期动脉期显示比周围健康肝组织更亮。如果增强在内部扩散且不局限于病灶外边缘，则称为"非边缘型"。只有表现出明确非边缘型APHE的病灶才能被考虑用于最高概率等级(LR-3至LR-5)。

如果存在非边缘型APHE，LI-RADS等级由病灶大小与非边缘型APHE与两个主要特征的相互作用的组合决定：第一，非周边廓清，病灶在门脉期或延迟期变暗，比肝脏暗。第二，增强型包膜，薄的周边缘由于对比剂滞留在门脉期或延迟期保持增强。病灶大小和一个或两个特征的存在增加了HCC的概率。例如，一个2.3厘米、具有非边缘型APHE且有一个额外特征(廓清或包膜)的病灶被分类为LR-5("明确HCC")，而没有非边缘型APHE的病灶最多只能达到LR-4("可能HCC")，即使它很大且表现出廓清。

## 挑战赛目标

通过根据这个经过临床验证的多分类诊断框架对模型进行基准测试，AMPLIFAI旨在加速开发可信且可解释的肝癌诊断AI系统，帮助缩小算法性能与真实临床应用之间的差距。

参赛者将获得由多阶段CT体积数据、病灶级LI-RADS分类标签和体素级分割组成的训练数据。模型将仅根据其预测正确LI-RADS等级的能力进行评估，有利于专注于稳健且临床相关的分类性能。

pages.title: Participation
pages.content:
## Eligibility and Registration
Pariticpiating Teams must register [here](https://forms.office.com/Pages/ResponsePage.aspx?id=pglwcd4gGkaIlAMSo5XKyWO--qpY16BHkqFYmo-jrRdURDM5R0c0VkZRMTM4S1lDQlo2MVZDT1VXTy4u), agree to the AMPLIFAI [Terms](https://github.com/UM2ii/amplifai-challenge/blob/main/terms-and-conditions.pdf) section, and apply for registration on the Codabench platform.
Approval will be communicated in the form of an email to the primary contact of the team.
pages.content_zh:
## 资格和注册

参赛团队必须在[这里](https://forms.office.com/Pages/ResponsePage.aspx?id=pglwcd4gGkaIlAMSo5XKyWO--qpY16BHkqFYmo-jrRdURDM5R0c0VkZRMTM4S1lDQlo2MVZDT1VXTy4u)进行注册，同意AMPLIFAI[条款](https://github.com/UM2ii/amplifai-challenge/blob/main/terms-and-conditions.pdf)部分，并在Codabench平台上申请注册。
批准信息将以电子邮件的形式发送至团队主要联系人。

pages.title: Challenge Data
pages.content:
## Challenge Data
The training and test datasets consist of multi-phase contrast-enhanced CT scans. All cases are annotated by three board-certified abdominal subspecialty radiologists, providing lesion-level LI-RADS category assignments and voxel-level segmentations for non-rim APHE, non-peripheral washout, and enhancing capsule. A case refers to a single patient containing all four contrast phases: non-contrast, arterial, portal venous, and delayed. All CT volumes and segmentations are provided in NIfTI format.

## Dataset Summary
#### Split - Cases
* Training Set - 601Cases
* Validation Set - 67 Cases
* Test Set (Private) - 257 Cases

The validation set comprises approximately 10% of the public data and is constructed to ensure equitable representation across all contributing datasets.

## Training and Validation Data
The public training and validation dataset was curated from four publicly accessible datasets and re-annotated for this challenge. The contributing datasets and their source institutions are listed below.

#### Dataset - Institution
* WAW-TACE - Medical University of Warsaw
* HCC-TACE-SEG - The University of Texas MD Anderson Cancer Center
* TCGA-LIHC - Mayo Clinic, Rochester; University of North Carolina, Chapel Hill; Alberta Health Services; Lahey Hospital & Medical Center
* PLC-CECT - Chongqing Yubei District People's Hospital

Cases from contributing datasets that included existing annotations were partially pre-annotated; however, all training and validation cases were fully re-annotated by three board-certified radiologists following the challenge protocol.

## Test Data
The held-out test set was curated within the University of Maryland Medical System and consists of 257 cases acquired between February 2023 and December 2025, drawn from three different imaging centers within the system. It was annotated following the same protocol as the training data. The test set is private and will not be released publicly; no additional information is provided to ensure the integrity of the challenge.

## Annotation Protocol
All studies were randomly shuffled and pre-loaded into a custom 3D Slicer module prior to annotation. Board-certified abdominal subspecialty radiologists used this module to manually segment liver lesions and record LI-RADS category assignments and feature-level annotations using standard Slicer tools integrated into the custom interface. A brief onboarding session demonstrated the workflow and annotation fields, after which the same protocol was applied uniformly across all dataset splits. Radiologists were blinded to dataset splits, and the same annotation protocol was applied uniformly across training, validation, and test cases.

Rather than algorithmically merging annotations, a structured cross-validation review was performed among the three radiologists: each radiologist's annotations were independently reviewed and corrected by the other two. Segmentations and labels were revised as needed during this process, and the consensus-validated result was treated as the final reference standard. This review procedure was applied consistently across training, validation, and test cases.
pages.content_zh:
## 挑战数据
训练和测试数据集由多期增强CT扫描组成。所有病例均由三名经过认证的腹部亚专科放射科医师标注，提供病灶级的LI-RADS分类和体素级的分割，包括非边缘动脉期肝特异性增强（non-rim APHE）、非周边延迟期廓清（non-peripheral washout）和增强包膜（enhancing capsule）。一个病例指单个患者的全部四个对比增强相位：非增强相、动脉相、门静脉相和延迟相。所有CT体积数据和分割结果以NIfTI格式提供。

## 数据集概况
#### 分割 - 病例数
* 训练集 - 601个病例
* 验证集 - 67个病例
* 测试集（私有） - 257个病例

验证集约占公开数据的10%，经过设计以确保所有贡献数据集的公平表示。

## 训练和验证数据
公开的训练和验证数据集由四个公开可获得的数据集合并而成，并针对本挑战进行了重新标注。贡献数据集及其来源机构如下列表所示。

#### 数据集 - 机构
* WAW-TACE - 华沙医科大学
* HCC-TACE-SEG - 德州大学MD安德森癌症中心
* TCGA-LIHC - 梅奥诊所罗切斯特分院；北卡罗来纳大学教堂山分校；艾伯塔卫生服务局；拉海医院与医疗中心
* PLC-CECT - 重庆渝北区人民医院

来自贡献数据集且包含现有标注的病例经过部分预标注；但所有训练和验证病例均由三名经过认证的放射科医师按照挑战协议完整重新标注。

## 测试数据
保留的测试集由马里兰大学医学系统内部策划，包含257个病例，采集时间为2023年2月至2025年12月，来自该系统内三个不同的成像中心。测试集按照与训练数据相同的协议进行了标注。测试集为私有数据，不会公开发布；为了确保挑战的完整性，不提供额外信息。

## 标注协议
所有研究均被随机打乱并预加载到自定义的3D Slicer模块中供标注使用。经过认证的腹部亚专科放射科医师使用此模块手动分割肝脏病灶，并使用集成到自定义界面中的标准Slicer工具记录LI-RADS分类、病灶级标注和特征级标注。放射科医师被隐瞒数据集分割信息，训练、验证和测试病例应用统一的标注协议。

而非采用算法方式合并标注，采用了结构化的交叉验证审查流程：三名放射科医师中的每一位的标注均由其他两位独立审查和修正。在此过程中根据需要修订分割结果和标签，共识验证的结果被视为最终参考标准。此审查程序在训练、验证和测试病例中一致应用。

pages.title: Evaluation
pages.content:
## How will submissions be evaluated?
Submissions will be ranked using Quadratic Weighted Kappa (QWK), a metric designed for ordinal classification that measures agreement between predicted and ground truth LI-RADS categories while penalizing larger disagreements more heavily. This is clinically appropriate: predicting LR-4 for a true LR-5 lesion is a less severe error than predicting LR-2.

The five ordinal categories considered are LR-1 ("definitely benign"), LR-2 ("probably benign"), LR-3 ("intermediate probability"), LR-4 ("probably HCC"), and LR-5 ("definitely HCC"). Lesions classified as LR-M are excluded from this task to preserve the ordinal structure of the classification problem.

QWK is the primary ranking metric; additional metrics will be reported for diagnostic purposes only and will not affect leaderboard standings. All submitted algorithms must produce a LI-RADS prediction for the single pre-defined target lesion in every test case. If a valid prediction is not generated for a given case, that case is excluded from the QWK calculation and a completion rate - defined as the percentage of test cases with valid predictions - is reported separately. Algorithms falling below 95% completion may be deemed non-compliant and excluded from final rankings and awards.
pages.content_zh:
## 提交内容如何评估？

提交的内容将使用二次加权卡帕系数（QWK）进行排名，这是一种为有序分类设计的指标，用于衡量预测的LI-RADS类别与真实标签之间的一致性，并对较大的偏差进行更严重的惩罚。这在临床上是合适的：将真实的LR-5病变预测为LR-4的错误比预测为LR-2的错误更为轻微。

考虑的五个有序类别为LR-1（"明确良性"）、LR-2（"可能良性"）、LR-3（"中等概率"）、LR-4（"可能HCC"）和LR-5（"明确HCC"）。分类为LR-M的病变被排除在本任务之外，以保持分类问题的有序结构。

QWK是主要排名指标；其他指标仅用于诊断目的，不影响排行榜排名。所有提交的算法必须为每个测试用例中预先定义的单个目标病变生成LI-RADS预测。如果某个用例未生成有效预测，该用例将被排除在QWK计算之外，并单独报告完成率（定义为具有有效预测的测试用例的百分比）。完成率低于95%的算法可能被认定为不符合要求，并被排除在最终排名和奖项之外。

pages.title: Challenge Dates and Deadlines
pages.content: **Challenge website and registration opens** – April 1, 2026 **Training dataset release** – June 1, 2026 **Team registration closes** – September 1, 2026 **Submission deadline** – September 15, 2026 **Challenge day** – October 1, 2026
pages.content_zh: **挑战赛网站和注册开放** – 2026年4月1日 **训练数据集发布** – 2026年6月1日 **团队注册截止** – 2026年9月1日 **提交截止日期** – 2026年9月15日 **挑战赛日** – 2026年10月1日
